title: Barn Bird House and Feeder
summary: A combination birdhouse and open feeder built from cedar fence boards under one shared peaked roof.
description:
Most birdhouse plans are one nesting box. This one is three functions under a single barn-shaped roof: a nesting box facing front, a second nesting box on one side, and an open feeder tray on the opposite side.

Every piece here comes off cheap cedar fence boards, so the **bevel angles do the shaping work** that would otherwise need a more expensive material — 22.5° and 45° bevels on ten small parts is what turns flat fence-board stock into a barn-roof silhouette.

The two roof/door panels are hinged rather than nailed shut, on purpose — a birdhouse nobody can open for an annual cleanout turns into a health hazard for the birds using it.

tools.note: Handles both the 22.5° and 45° bevels — set up a stop block to keep the small parts consistent.
Drills the entrance hole and dowel-perch hole.

materials.name: Cedar fence board, 5/8" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft

cutList.note: 45° angle. One gets a 1-1/2" entrance hole and a 3/8" dowel hole for the perch.
22.5° bevel. These hinge open for cleanout access.

steps.title: Cut all ten lettered parts
steps.body: Cut parts A through J to the sizes and bevel angles in the cut list. Set up a stop block on the miter saw for any pieces sharing a length — there are several — so they come out identical without re-measuring each one.
materials: Cedar fence board, 5/8" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft

steps.title: Build the main body shell
steps.body: Glue and nail the base panel (A) to the angled back panel (B) to form the core of the structure.
materials: Cedar fence board, 5/8" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft

steps.title: Add the corner pieces
steps.body: Attach the four angled corner pieces (C) at the front and side sections with glue and finish nails, following the angles cut in step one.

steps.title: Build the roof-slope panels
steps.body: Drill the entrance hole and the dowel-perch hole in one of the two roof-slope side panels (D) before attaching either — it's much easier to drill flat than after the panel is fastened in at an angle.
materials: Cedar fence board, 5/8" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft

steps.title: Fill in the wall panels
steps.body: Glue and nail the remaining wall panels (E, F, G, H, I) around the nesting compartments per the cut list, building out both the front nesting box and the side feeder opening.
materials: Cedar fence board, 5/8" x 5-1/2" x 6 ft

steps.title: Hinge the roof and insert the perch
steps.body:
Attach the two roof/door panels (J) with small hinges so the interior stays accessible for an annual cleanout, then glue the dowel perch into its hole.

Finish the exterior or leave it natural, and mount with hardware rated for outdoor exposure.
